HomeContent.howTitle: How to shorten a URL with UTM tracking
HomeContent.howIntro: iny.one is a free URL shortener that turns long links into short, trackable ones in three steps. Unlike most free shorteners, it builds UTM parameters and a QR code into the same flow, so a single short link works across email, social, and print campaigns.
HomeContent.step1: Paste the long URL you want to share into the shortener field.
HomeContent.step2: Optionally add UTM parameters (source, medium, campaign) to tag the link for analytics.
HomeContent.step3: Copy your short link, or download its QR code, and share it anywhere.
HomeContent.utmIntro: UTM parameters are tags added to the end of a URL that tell analytics tools where a visit came from. They are the standard way marketers attribute traffic to specific campaigns, channels, and ads in Google Analytics and similar platforms. iny.one adds them for you so every short link is measurable from the moment it is created.
HomeContent.utmSourceDesc: Identifies where the traffic comes from — for example google, facebook, or newsletter.
HomeContent.utmMediumDesc: Identifies the marketing medium — for example cpc, email, or social.
HomeContent.utmCampaignDesc: Identifies the specific campaign — for example summer_sale or product_launch.
HomeContent.qrTitle: Free QR codes for every short link
HomeContent.qrIntro: Every link you shorten with iny.one can be turned into a downloadable QR code at no cost. This lets the same tracked short link bridge digital and offline channels — posters, packaging, business cards, or event signage — while clicks and scans land in one analytics view. Many competing shorteners reserve QR codes or watermark-free downloads for paid plans.
HomeContent.privacyIntro: iny.one does not insert an advertising page before redirecting visitors, and basic shortening does not require an account — two things that several major shorteners changed on their free plans in recent years. No personal data is collected for basic redirection; shortened URLs are used only to send visitors to their destination.

AboutPage.title: About iny.one
AboutPage.metaTitle: About iny.one — Free URL Shortener with UTM & QR
AboutPage.metaDescription: Learn what iny.one is, how its free UTM tracking and QR codes work, and what's on the roadmap for this lightweight, privacy-focused URL shortener.
AboutPage.intro: iny.one is a free and lightweight URL shortener, designed for marketers, developers, and content creators who need to easily track campaigns using UTM parameters.
AboutPage.why: This project was born from the need to share clean, trackable links without relying on heavy platforms. I wanted to build a fast, privacy-focused and useful tool.
AboutPage.contact: Have ideas or found a bug? I’m open to feedback.

faqs.a: It’s a free tool to shorten URLs and add UTM parameters for better campaign tracking.
Yes, the service is completely free and does not require registration.
UTM parameters let you track the origin of web traffic in your marketing campaigns.

faqs.q: Can I see stats for my links?
faqs.a: Not yet, but we’re working on it.
We don’t collect or store personal data. Shortened URLs are used only for redirection.
